article_meta.title: Marchers protesting planned deportations block major freeway in Los Angeles 1 of 18 | Thousands of people protesting mas

reasoning: The model classifies this AP photo gallery/wire report as Center with 0.85 confidence. The article is indeed a minimal-prose AP report consisting mostly of photo captions with neutral descriptive language. The Center classification is defensible - the author's prose is factual and neutral. The key_indicators correctly cite the author's own prose. The only potential issue is whether 'mass deportations' could be considered slightly loaded (some might argue it's Lean Left framing), but it's also a factually accurate description of the policy. The confidence of 0.85 is reasonable for a piece with clear neutral signals. No attribution violations are present.

custom_reasoning.classification_accuracy: The article is an AP wire report/photo gallery with minimal prose, all of which is neutral and factual. Center is a defensible classification. One could argue Lean Left based on the framing choice of 'mass deportations' and the one-sided coverage of only the protest perspective without any government/enforcement perspective, but Center is within one adjacent class of Lean Left at worst.
custom_reasoning.confidence_calibration: 0.85 confidence for a Center classification of a minimal-prose AP wire report is reasonable. The signals of neutrality are clear - neutral verbs, factual descriptions, no loaded language. The confidence appropriately reflects the clarity of the neutral signal.
custom_reasoning.reasoning_evidence_quality: All three key_indicators cite the author's own prose. The reasoning correctly identifies the neutral verb choices and factual descriptions. No attribution violations. The evidence is specific and directly quoted from the article.
